items.text:
Hi Marco,

Thanks for signing up to volunteer as a campus tour guide this spring. Your first shift is confirmed for Saturday, March 14, from 10:00 AM to 12:30 PM at the Visitor Center. Lunch will be provided afterward, and you'll receive a $25 stipend.

Best,
Admissions Office

questions.stem: According to the email, when is Marco's first tour guide shift?

options.A: Saturday, March 14, from 10:00 AM to 12:30 PM.
options.B: Sunday, March 14, from 10:30 AM to 12:00 PM.
options.C: Saturday, March 13, from 10:00 AM to 12:30 PM.
options.D: Sunday, March 15, from 10:30 AM to 12:00 PM.

questions.explanation: The email confirms the shift for 'Saturday, March 14, from 10:00 AM to 12:30 PM at the Visitor Center.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the email about Marco?

options.A: He has volunteered for many tours before.
options.B: He paid a $25 fee to volunteer.
options.C: He is expected to bring his own lunch.
options.D: He will be compensated for his volunteer time.

questions.explanation: The email states 'you'll receive a $25 stipend,' implying Marco will be paid for his time.

items.text: NOTICE: Elevator B in Whitman Hall will be out of service from Monday, April 6 through Friday, April 10 for scheduled maintenance. Please use Elevator A on the north side of the building or the central stairwell. Repairs will be completed by 6:00 PM Friday.

questions.stem: According to the notice, where is the alternative elevator located?

options.A: On the south side of the building.
options.B: On the north side of the building.
options.C: On the east end of the building.
options.D: On the west wing of the building.

questions.explanation: The notice directs readers to 'use Elevator A on the north side of the building.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the notice about Whitman Hall?

options.A: It will be closed for the entire week.
options.B: It has more than one working elevator.
options.C: It will undergo a major structural renovation.
options.D: It cannot be accessed by the central stairwell.

questions.explanation: The notice tells readers to 'use Elevator A,' implying the building contains another working elevator besides Elevator B.

items.text:
Hi Aisha,

Your fall meal plan ended on December 15 with an unused balance of $42.30. This amount will be refunded to your student account by January 8. To check the refund status, log in to the Dining Portal after that date.

Best,
Campus Dining

questions.stem: According to the email, how will the balance be returned to Aisha?

options.A: As a refund to her student account.
options.B: As a cash refund at Campus Dining.
options.C: As a credit toward next semester's plan.
options.D: As a refund to her Dining Portal.

questions.explanation: The email states the balance 'will be refunded to your student account by January 8.'

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the email about the Dining Portal?

options.A: It charges a small fee for refund checks.
options.B: It tracks payments made during fall semester.
options.C: It will display the refund after January 8.
options.D: It opens only during the academic year.

questions.explanation: The email instructs Aisha to 'log in to the Dining Portal after that date' to check the refund status, implying the refund appears after Jan 8.

items.text:
Hi Damian,

This is a reminder that your mandatory lab safety training is scheduled for Wednesday, April 22, at 2:00 PM in Room 305 of Hayes Hall. Please bring the $15 materials fee in cash. Skipping the session will defer your lab access by one month.

Best,
Safety Office

questions.stem: According to the email, where will the safety training take place?
questions.explanation: The email states the training is scheduled 'in Room 305 of Hayes Hall.'

questions.stem: The word "defer" in the email is closest in meaning to
questions.explanation: In context, 'defer your lab access by one month' means to postpone or delay access, matching 'delay.'
